title: Multi-Provider Management

use_cases.name: Select Default Provider
use_cases.description: Developer sets ai.default config key; AiManager::getDefaultInstance() reads it, returning provider name or Lab enum value.

use_cases.name: Switch Provider at Runtime
use_cases.description: Developer calls setDefaultInstance($name) to override the active provider without restarting the app.

use_cases.name: Resolve Provider by Name
use_cases.description: Developer calls instance($name) or a typed accessor; AiManager reads ai.providers.$name config via getInstanceConfig(), instantiates correct driver.

use_cases.name: Create Text Provider
use_cases.description: Developer requests a TextProvider; AiManager validates capability, throws LogicException if unsupported.

use_cases.name: Use Web Search Tool
use_cases.description: Provider must implement SupportsWebSearch; each provider formats options differently.

use_cases.name: Fake Provider for Testing
use_cases.description: AiManager uses Concerns\InteractsWithFake* traits; faked providers clone real provider and swap gateway with fake.

use_cases.name: Compose Provider with Agent
use_cases.description: textProviderFor(Agent $agent) returns provider with fake gateway injected if agent has a registered fake.

factory_methods.createOllamaDriver: OllamaProvider(config, Dispatcher) — no injected gateway; lazily creates OllamaGateway

key_relationships: All providers extend abstract Provider (has name(), driver(), providerCredentials(), additionalConfiguration())
Provider contracts: TextProvider, ImageProvider, AudioProvider, EmbeddingProvider, TranscriptionProvider, FileProvider, StoreProvider, RerankingProvider
Providers use trait composition for capabilities (Concerns\GeneratesText, Concerns\HasTextGateway, etc.)
AiManager performs capability validation via instanceof checks before returning typed providers
AiManager throws LogicException if provider does not implement requested capability interface
runtime_switching_flow: 1. Config: ai.default = 'openai' (or any provider name / Lab enum)
2. AiManager::textProvider() → AiManager::instance(null) → resolves default → calls createOpenaiDriver($config)
5. Named access: AiManager::textProvider('gemini') → bypasses default, resolves 'gemini' directly
important_notes: AiManager is the single entry point — developer never instantiates providers directly. Capability gating is enforced at runtime via instanceof checks. Ollama/Mistral/Groq lazily create their own gateways; OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini receive gateways via constructor injection from AiManager. All providers support configurable model tiers: defaultTextModel, cheapestTextModel, smartestTextModel.
